timeline.title: Election Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Key milestones in a General Election cycle

announcement.desc: Election Commission announces election dates. Model Code of Conduct kicks in immediately — government cannot announce new schemes or transfers.

nominations.desc: Candidates file their nomination papers. The Returning Officer scrutinises each nomination for eligibility and completeness.

withdrawal.desc: Last date for candidates to withdraw from the race. The final candidate list is published and symbols are allotted.

campaign.desc: Parties campaign actively through rallies, advertising, and canvassing. Model Code of Conduct is strictly enforced throughout.

silence.desc: 48 hours before polling, all campaigning stops. No rallies, no political ads, no social media campaigns during this period.

polling.desc: Voters cast their ballots. Booths open 7 AM to 6 PM. Indelible ink marks your finger. Carry at least one approved photo ID.

counting.desc: Ballots are counted at designated counting centres under observer supervision. Results are declared and winners take oath of office.

simulation.title: Voting Simulation
simulation.instruction: Select your candidate and press the blue button
simulation.successMessage: Your vote has been successfully cast.

boothLocator.title: Find Your Polling Booth
boothLocator.searchPlaceholder: Enter your name or EPIC number
boothLocator.noResults: No booth found. Please check your details.
boothLocator.caption: Polling Booth
boothLocator.pincodeDesc: Enter your 6-digit pincode to locate your nearest polling booth.
boothLocator.smsAlternative: Alternatively, SMS {code} to {number} or call the Voter Helpline.

forms.caption: Electoral Forms Explained
forms.title: Every Form You Need to Know

form6.title: New Voter Registration
form6.eligibility: Indian citizen, 18+ years old by January 1 of the reference year

form7.title: Deletion of Voter Entry
form7.eligibility: Any citizen can file on behalf of a deceased or relocated voter

form8.title: Correction in Voter Details
form8.eligibility: Any registered voter who needs to update their electoral entry

form8a.title: Transposition Within Constituency
form8a.eligibility: Voters who have moved to a new address within the same Assembly constituency

epic.title: Verify Your Voter ID Number
epic.invalidFormat: Format: 3 capital letters followed by 7 digits
epic.description: Your EPIC number is printed on your Voter ID card. It has 3 capital letters followed by 7 digits.
epic.caption: Voter ID Tool

problems.caption: Common Issues
problems.title: Got a Problem? We've Got the Fix

name_mismatch.problem: Name on voter card doesn't match Aadhaar
name_mismatch.solution: File Form 8 for name correction. Upload your Aadhaar as proof of the correct name.

not_in_list.problem: My name is missing from the electoral roll
not_in_list.solution: Search at voters.eci.gov.in to confirm. If genuinely missing, file Form 6 immediately.

wrong_photo.problem: My voter ID has a very old or blurry photo
wrong_photo.solution: File Form 8 for photo correction. Bring a new recent passport-size photograph.

double_entry.problem: I'm registered in two different constituencies
double_entry.solution: File Form 7 to delete one entry. By law, you can only be registered in one constituency.

wrong_address.problem: Voter ID shows my old address after moving
wrong_address.solution: If within the same constituency, use Form 8A (transposition — fastest option).

damaged_epic.problem: My Voter ID card is damaged or torn
damaged_epic.solution: Apply for a duplicate EPIC at voters.eci.gov.in. Pay the Rs 25 fee.

metadata.title: VoteGuide India — Your Complete Voter Information Assistant
metadata.description: Understand voter registration (Form 6), corrections (Form 8), and how to vote in India.
